Class SigNozLogProcessor

java.lang.Object
javax.annotation.processing.AbstractProcessor
io.signoz.springboot.processor.SigNozLogProcessor
All Implemented Interfaces:
Processor
@SupportedAnnotationTypes("io.signoz.springboot.annotation.SigNozLog") @SupportedSourceVersion(RELEASE_8) public class SigNozLogProcessor extends AbstractProcessor
Annotation processor that injects a private static final SLF4J Logger field into every class annotated with SigNozLog.

On Java 8: Uses com.sun.tools.javac internal APIs (available via tools.jar) to modify the AST directly, injecting: 

  private static final org.slf4j.Logger log =
      org.slf4j.LoggerFactory.getLogger(ThisClass.class);

On Java 9–21: The same internal APIs are accessible when the consumer's build configures --add-opens for jdk.compiler. The starter's POM configures these automatically for consuming projects.

Graceful fallback: If the internal APIs are inaccessible (GraalVM native-image, restricted security policy, etc.), the processor emits a WARNING and skips the injection. The developer can then declare private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyClass.class); manually — all SigNoz enrichment still applies via Logback configuration.
